records.ety: [F. compiler, fr.L. compilare to plunder, pillage; com- + pilare to plunder. See Pill, v. t., Pillage.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Compiled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Compiling.]

defs: To put together; to construct; to build.
quotes: Before that Merlin died, he did intend A brazen wall in compass to compile.

defs: To put together in a new form out of materials already existing; esp., to put together or compose out of materials from other books or documents.
quotes: He [Goldsmith] compiled for the use of schools a History of Rome.

defs: to process (computer program source code) with a compiler{2} to produce an assembly-language program or an executable program in machine language.
